questions.question: A bacterial colony starts with 500 cells and triples every 2 hours. How many cells will be present after 6 hours?
questions.explanation: After 6 hours, there are 6/2 = 3 doubling periods. Cells = 500 × 3³ = 500 × 27 = 13,500.

questions.question: The value of a car depreciates according to V(t) = 25,000(0.85)^t, where t is measured in years. By what percent does the car's value decrease each year?
questions.explanation: The decay factor is 0.85, meaning 85% of the value remains. So 100% − 85% = 15% is lost each year.

questions.question: An investment of $2,000 grows at an annual rate of 4% per year. Write an expression for the value after n years, assuming compound annual interest.
questions.explanation: Compound growth formula is A = P(1 + r)^t. Here P = 2000 and r = 0.04, so A = 2000(1.04)^n.

questions.question: A radioactive substance decays so that half of it remains after every 3 days. If you start with 80 grams, how much remains after 9 days?
questions.explanation: In 9 days there are 9/3 = 3 half-lives. After 3 half-lives: 80 × (1/2)³ = 80 × 1/8 = 10 grams.

questions.question: The population of a city increases by 2.5% each year. If the current population is 150,000, what will it be after 2 years? Round to the nearest whole number.
A scientist observes that a sample of bacteria follows the model N(t) = 1200(2)^(t/5), where t is in minutes. At what time t will the population reach 9,600 bacteria?
questions.explanation: Set 1200(2)^(t/5) = 9600. Divide by 1200: (2)^(t/5) = 8 = 2³. So t/5 = 3, giving t = 15 minutes.
